rationale: Beginner educational overview of 18 standard chart patterns (ascending/descending triangle, pennant, wedge, rounding top/bottom, cup and handle). Each pattern is described with basic entry, stop-loss, and target rules, but the content is generic technical-analysis education — no proprietary or novel mechanical claims, no performance data, and no falsifiable edge. The speaker uses probabilistic language ('chances of the market falling/rising') throughout, confirming an instructional rather than research-intake purpose.

claims.summary: Ascending triangle: continuation bullish pattern forming in a prior uptrend. Resistance is flat (R1=R2) while support rises (S1<S2<S3). Breakout above resistance is the entry trigger.
claims.trigger_rule: Price breaks above the flat resistance level after at least two touches
claims.invalidation_rule: Price breaks below the rising support trendline before the breakout
required_data: OHLCV bars sufficient to identify swing highs/lows
missing_fields: minimum bar count for pattern formation

claims.summary: Bullish pennant: entry in the candle after the breakout candle. Stop-loss below the breakout candle. Target equals the height of the preceding pole.
claims.trigger_rule: Price breaks above the converging support/resistance lines of the pennant after at least two touches each side
claims.invalidation_rule: Price breaks below the lower trendline of the pennant
required_data: OHLCV bars showing prior uptrend pole and the converging pennant

claims.summary: Cup and handle: rounding bottom (cup) followed by a smaller rounding bottom (handle) below the cup's rim. A breakout above the rim resistance is the buy signal. A second touch of the rim strengthens the pattern.
claims.trigger_rule: Price breaks above the resistance at the cup's rim after forming the handle
claims.invalidation_rule: Not explicitly stated in the source
required_data: OHLCV bars showing the full cup and handle shape
